(Spanish: [[baraŋkaβeɾˈmexa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a municipality and city in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), located on the shore of the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River"), in the western part of the department of [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department"). It is home to the largest oil refinery in the country, under direct management of [ECOPETROL](/wiki/Ecopetrol "Ecopetrol"). Barrancabermeja is known as the Oil Capital of Colombia.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**Barrancabermeja** (Spanish: [[baraŋkaβeɾˈmexa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a municipality and city in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), located on the shore of the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River"), in the western part of the department of [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department"). It is home to the largest oil refinery in the country, under direct management of [ECOPETROL](/wiki/Ecopetrol "Ecopetrol"). Barrancabermeja is known as the Oil Capital of Colombia.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

It is located 114 km west of [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga"), on the banks of the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River") in the [Middle Magdalena](/w/index.php?title=Middle_Magdalena&action=edit&redlink=1 "Middle Magdalena (page does not exist)") region, which is the largest municipality and second in the entire department.

Barrancabermeja has high temperatures all year round. It is considered to be one of the hottest cities in Colombia. It is surrounded by water resources which make it very humid.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barrancabermeja&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

On February 23, 2022, Barrancabermeja was affected by a 5.5 [Richter Scale](/wiki/Richter_Scale "Richter Scale") magnitude earthquake.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barrancabermeja&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[Puerto Wilches](/wiki/Puerto_Wilches "Puerto Wilches") lies to the north of Barrancabermeja; Puerto Parra and Simacota to the south; San Vicente de Chucuri and Giron to the southeast; and Magdalena River to the west. It is 116 km from Bucaramanga, the capital of Santander.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
The city is situated on the banks of the Magdalena River. In the past a Ferry service would cross between Santander and Antioquia, on the opposite side, where further oil fields are found. A bridge now spans the river at this location.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barrancabermeja&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

Barrancabermeja has a [tropical monsoon climate](/wiki/Tropical_monsoon_climate "Tropical monsoon climate") (Am) with heavy rainfall in all months except January.

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barrancabermeja&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Culture")]

Barrancabermeja's culture has been largely affected by the migratory movements caused by the oil boom. The city exhibits an amalgam of many regional customs from Colombia. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Due to its history as an oil town, the city is often visited by moneyed foreigners, often engineers and supervisory employees and technicians of oil companies. Thus Barrancabermeja has a reputation as an open and vibrant city and may be somewhat cosmopolitan.

Barrancabermeja's night life includes [Salsa](/wiki/Salsa_(dance) "Salsa (dance)") and [merengue](/wiki/Merengue_(dance) "Merengue (dance)") dance clubs. The city's culture presents a strong Caribbean influence that nevertheless is not exclusive of other cultural expressions from among the variety found in Colombia. The predominant music in the city is [vallenato](/wiki/Vallenato "Vallenato") and other Caribbean rhythms including [papayera](/w/index.php?title=Papayera&action=edit&redlink=1 "Papayera (page does not exist)").

Several local and national political organizations including unions, women's organizations, and human rights groups are active. Marches and demonstrations are quite common and the oil workers union is among the most important in the nation.

The commercial area of the city is adjacent to the river port ("the Port") where motor boats and [canoes](/wiki/Canoe "Canoe") constantly arrive with [fish](/wiki/Fish "Fish") and agricultural products from farmers along the river that include [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), [cassava](/wiki/Cassava "Cassava") (or *yuca* in Spanish), [plantain](/wiki/Plantain_(cooking) "Plantain (cooking)"), and [cacao](/wiki/Cocoa_bean "Cocoa bean").

In 1996 and 2000, Barrancabermeja hosted the Mundial de Patinaje, an international inline-skating competition.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barrancabermeja&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transportation")]

The city is served by [Yariguíes Airport](/wiki/Yarigu%C3%ADes_Airport "Yariguíes Airport").
